methodological_ground.detail: Pseudo-gap rejection rule is a strong methodological safeguard

code_usability.detail: No code generated; Category 1 evidence analysis only

reliability.note: Step 2 mandates live retrieval (PubMed/Google Scholar) but no fallback for offline execution

agent_usability.note: Same live-retrieval inconsistency as other evidence skills

agent_specific.note: Pseudo-gap rejection section is a strong differentiator; gap-to-study conversion adds direct value

inputs.label: Find research gaps in ferroptosis and diabetic kidney disease

assertions.text: Evidence landscape audit produced before any formal gap claim
Pseudo-gaps rejected with explicit rationale
Only medium/high-confidence gaps enter final gap map
Gap-to-study conversion table produced for top gaps
No fabricated PMIDs, DOIs, or study findings as gap evidence

inputs.label: Single-cell COPD gaps with one publishable direction recommendation

assertions.text: Primary recommended direction justified on novelty-feasibility-impact balance
Evidence landscape crowding accurately characterized before gap claims
Generic 'add single-cell' suggestion rejected as pseudo-gap unless tied to unresolved question
Self-critical risk review present
Preprint evidence separated from peer-reviewed evidence

inputs.label: Immunotherapy resistance gaps in HCC with anchor papers provided

assertions.text: Anchor papers used to map covered territory before gap identification
Direct-topic evidence distinguished from adjacent transferable evidence
Saturated areas plainly identified without pretending broad novelty
Gap confidence levels (High/Medium/Low) assigned to all identified gaps
No fabricated study findings used to support gap claims

inputs.label: Very sparse field with few existing studies — only low-confidence gaps available

assertions.text: Low-confidence gaps not elevated to priority opportunity status
Few studies correctly not equated with important publishable gap
Evidence uncertainty explicitly stated
Recommendation appropriately conservative in sparse-evidence context
Fallback path suggested if top gap collapses on closer review
assertions.note: Self-critical review section lacked explicit fallback path for sparse-field scenario

inputs.label: Gap analysis with request to exclude generic pseudo-gaps and focus on narrow unresolved questions

assertions.text: All generic upgrade suggestions rejected and listed in Section D (Pseudo-Gaps Rejected)
Remaining gaps are topic-specific and tied to demonstrated unresolved questions
Narrow follow-up study design specified per gap
More validation not listed as a strong gap by itself
Primary recommended direction stated with justification superior to alternatives

key_strengths: Mandatory pseudo-gap rejection with explicit listing (Section D) is an outstanding quality safeguard that prevents generic future-direction outputs
Nine-type gap taxonomy provides a comprehensive and systematic gap classification framework
Gap-to-study conversion table bridges gap identification and actionable study design
Hard rule 'No retrieval, no gap claim' enforces evidence-grounded analysis at the highest level

recommendations.title: Step 2 live retrieval requirement creates inconsistency in offline environments
recommendations.problem: Step 2 requires running literature retrieval via PubMed and Google Scholar before any gap claim. In offline execution this defaults to training memory, violating the hard rule 'No retrieval, no gap claim'.
recommendations.root_cause: The retrieval instruction assumes live search access without defining a fallback.
recommendations.fix: Add explicit fallback: 'When live retrieval is unavailable, label all evidence in Section B as [Based on training knowledge — not live retrieval]. Run a sample search string to help the user verify and supplement. Confidence levels for all gaps must be downgraded by one tier when formal retrieval was not completed.'

recommendations.title: Self-critical review lacks explicit fallback path in sparse-evidence scenarios
recommendations.problem: When a field is very sparse, the self-critical review identifies the weakest gap but does not always provide a concrete fallback path if the recommended direction collapses.
recommendations.root_cause: Step 8 asks for 'fallback path if the top gap collapses' but does not define a minimum fallback structure for sparse-field contexts.
recommendations.fix: Add a sparse-field fallback template to Step 8: if the field is sparse and all gaps are low-confidence, recommend either (1) a pilot exploratory study with explicit hypothesis-generating label, or (2) a systematic review/scoping review as a more appropriate first step.

recommendations.title: Gap-to-study conversion table lacks complexity/resource requirement dimension
recommendations.problem: The gap-to-study conversion table shows research style and minimal executable plan but does not include a resource requirement or feasibility indicator.
recommendations.root_cause: references/gap-to-study-conversion.md likely defines the table structure without a resource column.
recommendations.fix: Add a 'Resource Level' or 'Feasibility Tier' column to the gap-to-study conversion table: Low/Medium/High, based on whether the study requires only public data, prospective collection, or experimental resources.
